scores.correctness_notes: Gets event.fail() semantics right (no execution stop, user-visible message). However, mischaracterizes exception behavior: claims 'Ops catches it and marks the action as failed with a generic internal error', whereas the gold standard states an unhandled exception causes the charm to crash and puts the entire unit into error state, requiring 'juju resolve' to recover. This is a meaningful factual difference.
scores.hallucination_notes: The claim that Ops catches unhandled exceptions and marks only the action as failed (rather than crashing the unit into error state) is inaccurate per the gold standard — it understates the severity. Not a fabricated API, but a materially wrong behavioral description. All API names used (event.fail(), event.set_results(), ActionEvent) are real.
